zh.Little Chart, Kent, England, United King: 英國英格蘭肯特蕾托 哈特
zh.lifeSummary: Cecilia de Leigh是Marcellus de Leigh和Anne De Bridge的女兒，出生在英國。她與Valentine Barret在英國肯特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1442年3月11日，埋葬在英國肯特法維沙安。

zh-hans.Little Chart, Kent, England, United King: 英国英格兰肯特蕾托 哈特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Cecilia de Leigh是Marcellus de Leigh和Anne De Bridge的女儿，出生在英国。她与Valentine Barret在英国肯特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1442年3月11日，埋葬在英国肯特法維沙安。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Leigh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places (in at least sixteen counties, but especially Leigh in Lancashire) named either with the nominative case of Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’ (see Lee ) or with lēage, a late dative form of this word (see Lye ).
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 李, based on its Cantonese pronunciation. See   Li   1.

variants.name: Leah
meanings: Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 李 and 利, see   Li   1 and 3.

variants.name: Lee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sang, Jae, Jong, Jung, Sung, Yong, Kyung, Seung, Dong, Kwang, Myung.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a meadow or a patch of arable land, Middle English lee, lea, from Old English lēa, dative case (used after a preposition) of lēah, which originally meant ‘wood or glade’.

English: habitational name from any of the many places in England named with Old English lēah ‘wood, glade’, including Lee in Buckinghamshire, Essex, Hampshire, Kent, and Shropshire, and Lea in Cheshire, Derbyshire, Herefordshire, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, and Wiltshire.

nameMeanings.name: Cecilia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin name Caecilia, feminine form of Caecilius ( see Cecil ). This was a good deal more common than the masculine form, largely due to the fame of the 2nd- or 3rd-century virgin martyr whose name is still mentioned daily in the Roman Catholic Canon of the Mass. She is regarded as the patron saint of music and has inspired works such as Purcell's ‘Ode on St Cecilia's Day’, although the reasons for this association are not clear.

variants.name: Cecelia
meanings: From the Latin name Caecilia, feminine form of Caecilius ( see Cecil ). This was a good deal more common than the masculine form, largely due to the fame of the 2nd- or 3rd-century virgin martyr whose name is still mentioned daily in the Roman Catholic Canon of the Mass. She is regarded as the patron saint of music and has inspired works such as Purcell's ‘Ode on St Cecilia's Day’, although the reasons for this association are not clear.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Cecilia de Leigh was born in England as the daughter of Marcellus de Leigh and Anne De Bridge. She married Valentine Barret in Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 11 March 1442, and was buried in Faversham, Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Cecilia de Leigh est née à Angleterre, fille de Marcellus de Leigh et Anne De Bridge. Elle a épousé Valentine Barret à Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 11 mars 1442, et a été enterrée à Faversham, Kent, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Cecilia de Leigh wurde in England, als Tochter von Marcellus de Leigh und Anne De Bridge geboren. Sie hat in Kent, England Valentine Barret geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 11. März 1442 und wurde in Faversham, Kent, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Cecilia de LeighはイギリスでMarcellus de LeighとAnne De Bridgeの娘として生まれた。イギリスケントでValentine Barretと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1442年3月11日に死去し，イギリスケントファバーシャムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Cecilia de Leigh(은)는 영국에서 Marcellus de Leigh(와)과 Anne De Bridge의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 영국 켄트에서 Valentine Barret(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1442년 3월 11일 사망했으며 영국 켄트 파버샤에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Cecilia de Leigh nasceu em Inglaterra filha de Marcellus de Leigh e Anne De Bridge. Ela casou-se com Valentine Barret em Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 11 de março de 1442, e foi sepultada em Faversham, Kent, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Cecilia de Leigh родилась в местности Англия. Её родителей звали Marcellus de Leigh и Anne De Bridge. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Valentine Barret в местности кэнт, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 11 марта 1442 г. и была похоронена в местности фавиршам, кэнт, Англия.
